example.sentence: 失敗しても気を取り直して前に進もう。
example.meaning: Even if you fail, let's pull ourselves together and move forward.

113.meaning: to be considerate, to take care of someone's feelings
113.subject: 기초 동사

example.sentence: 彼はいつも周りの人に気を使う。
example.meaning: He is always considerate of the people around him.

113.meaning: to be discouraged, to feel down
113.subject: 기초 동사

example.sentence: 不合格でも気を落とさないでください。
example.meaning: Please don't be discouraged even if you fail.

example.sentence: 気持ちを言葉にするのは難しい。
example.meaning: It is difficult to put feelings into words.

example.sentence: 悲しんでいる友達に優しい言葉をかけた。
example.meaning: I said kind words to my sad friend.

example.sentence: 大切な場面では言葉を選んで話す。
example.meaning: In important situations, I choose my words carefully when speaking.

114.meaning: to be at a loss for words
114.subject: 언어 표현

example.sentence: 突然の質問に言葉に詰まってしまった。
example.meaning: I was at a loss for words due to the sudden question.

example.sentence: あまりの美しさに言葉を失った。
example.meaning: I was speechless at the breathtaking beauty.

example.sentence: 久しぶりに会った友達と言葉が弾んだ。
example.meaning: I had a lively conversation with a friend I hadn't seen in a long time.
example.meaningKo: 오랜만에 만난 친구와 대화가 활기차게 이어졌다.

example.sentence: 彼はいつも言葉を守る人だ。
example.meaning: He is a person who always keeps his word.

example.sentence: 彼は約束の言葉を破って信頼を失った。
example.meaning: He broke his word and lost trust.
example.meaningKo: 그는 약속의 말을 어겨 신뢰를 잃었다.

example.sentence: 友達の悩みの話を聞いてあげた。
example.meaning: I listened to my friend's worries.

example.sentence: 先生と将来について話をした。
example.meaning: I had a talk with my teacher about the future.

example.sentence: 毎日運動する時間を取るようにしている。
example.meaning: I try to make time to exercise every day.
example.meaningKo: 매일 운동할 시간을 내려고 하고 있다.

example.sentence: 気がつくと、あっという間に時間が経っていた。
example.meaning: Before I knew it, time had passed in the blink of an eye.
example.meaningKo: 정신을 차려보니 순식간에 시간이 지나 있었다.

example.sentence: この料理は時間をかけて丁寧に作った。
example.meaning: I made this dish carefully, spending a lot of time on it.
example.meaningKo: 이 요리는 시간을 들여 정성껏 만들었다.

example.sentence: 忙しい中でも読書する時間を作っている。
example.meaning: Even when busy, I find time to read.
example.meaningKo: 바쁜 와중에도 독서할 시간을 만들고 있다.

example.sentence: 試験勉強に時間が足りなくて困っている。
example.meaning: I'm struggling because I don't have enough time to study for the exam.

example.sentence: どんなに忙しくても締め切りを守ることが大切だ。
example.meaning: No matter how busy you are, it is important to meet your deadlines.
example.meaningKo: 아무리 바빠도 마감을 지키는 것이 중요하다.

example.sentence: 旅行に行く前に、しっかり予定を立てた。
example.meaning: Before going on the trip, I made a solid plan.
example.meaningKo: 여행을 가기 전에 제대로 예정을 세웠다.

example.sentence: 来月のスケジュールを組んでおく必要がある。
example.meaning: I need to draw up next month's schedule in advance.
example.meaningKo: 다음 달 스케줄을 미리 짜 둘 필요가 있다.

example.sentence: 急いだおかげで、電車に間に合った。
example.meaning: Thanks to hurrying, I made it in time for the train.
example.meaningKo: 서둔 덕분에 전철을 제때 탈 수 있었다.

115.meaning: to make up for lost time
115.subject: 시간 관리

example.sentence: 病気で休んだ分の遅れを取り戻すために頑張った。
example.meaning: I worked hard to make up for the time lost due to illness.
example.meaningKo: 아파서 쉰 만큼의 지연을 만회하기 위해 열심히 했다.

116.meaning: to lend one's strength; to help
116.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 困っている友達に力を貸した。
example.meaning: I lent my strength to a friend in need.

example.sentence: みんなで力を合わせて問題を解決した。
example.meaning: Everyone joined forces to solve the problem.

116.meaning: to relax one's strength; to ease up
116.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 肩の力を抜いてリラックスしてください。
example.meaning: Please relax and ease the tension in your shoulders.
furigana: リラックスしてください

116.meaning: to put effort into; to focus on
116.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 彼は英語の勉強に力を入れている。
example.meaning: He is putting a lot of effort into studying English.
example.meaningKo: 그는 영어 공부에 힘을 쏟고 있다.

116.meaning: to be of help; to be a support
116.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 何かあればいつでも力になるよ。
example.meaning: If anything happens, I'll always be here to help.
example.meaningKo: 무슨 일이 있으면 언제든지 힘이 돼줄게.

116.meaning: to feel motivated; to get into the mood to do something
116.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 先生に褒められてやる気になった。
example.meaning: I got motivated after being praised by my teacher.

116.meaning: to lose motivation; to lose the will to do something
116.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 失敗が続いてやる気をなくしてしまった。
example.meaning: I lost my motivation after repeated failures.

116.meaning: food and medicine share the same origin; let food be thy medicine
116.subject: 사자성어·표현

example.sentence: 医食同源というように、毎日の食事が健康の基本だ。
example.meaning: As the saying 'food is medicine' goes, daily meals are the foundation of health.

116.meaning: to do one's utmost; to give everything one has
116.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 試合で全力を尽くして戦った。
example.meaning: I gave everything I had in the match.

116.meaning: to do one's best; to give one's best effort
116.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 結果がどうであれ、ベストを尽くすことが大切だ。
example.meaning: Regardless of the result, it is important to do your best.
example.meaningKo: 결과가 어떻든 최선을 다하는 것이 중요하다.

example.sentence: 毎日練習すれば、日本語が上手くなる。
example.meaning: If you practice every day, your Japanese will improve.

example.sentence: 練習を続けると、どんどん上達する。
example.meaning: If you keep practicing, you will improve more and more.

example.sentence: 勉強がどんどん進む。
example.meaning: The studying progresses more and more.

example.sentence: サボってばかりいると、クラスに遅れる。
example.meaning: If you keep skipping, you will fall behind the class.
furigana: サボってばかりいると、

example.sentence: 頑張れば、みんなに追いつく。
example.meaning: If you work hard, you can catch up with everyone.
example.meaningKo: 열심히 하면 모두를 따라잡을 수 있다.

example.sentence: 勉強すると、漢字がわかるようになる。
example.meaning: If you study, you will come to understand kanji.

example.sentence: 練習すると、泳げるできるようになる。
example.meaning: If you practice, you will become able to swim.
example.meaningKo: 연습하면 수영을 할 수 있게 된다.
furigana: げるできるようになる

example.sentence: 慣れると、敬語が使えるようになる。
example.meaning: Once you get used to it, you will become able to use honorific language.
example.meaningKo: 익숙해지면 경어를 쓸 수 있게 된다.

117.meaning: to acquire / to become second nature
117.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 繰り返し練習すると、スキルが身につく。
example.meaning: If you practice repeatedly, the skill will become second nature.

example.sentence: 新しい言語を身につけるには時間がかかる。
example.meaning: It takes time to acquire a new language.
example.meaningKo: 새로운 언어를 습득하는 데는 시간이 걸린다.

example.sentence: 毎日同じ食事をしていると、飽きが来る。
example.meaning: If you eat the same meal every day, you get tired of it.
example.meaningKo: 매일 같은 식사를 하다 보면 싫증이 난다.

118.meaning: to not last; to not continue
118.subject: 동작 표현

example.sentence: 忙しいと、運動が続かない。
example.meaning: When you are busy, exercise does not last.

118.meaning: to keep going without getting bored
118.subject: 동작 표현

example.sentence: 好きなことなら、飽きずに続けられる。
example.meaning: If it is something you like, you can keep going without getting bored.
example.meaningKo: 좋아하는 것이라면 질리지 않고 계속할 수 있다.

example.sentence: 日記を毎日続けることが大切だ。
example.meaning: It is important to continue writing a diary every day.
example.meaningKo: 일기를 매일 계속 쓰는 것이 중요하다.

example.sentence: 朝起きたらストレッチをする癖にする。
example.meaning: I make it a habit to stretch when I wake up in the morning.
example.meaningKo: 아침에 일어나면 스트레칭을 하는 습관으로 삼는다.

example.sentence: 夜のスキンケアをルーティンにする。
example.meaning: I make my nighttime skincare a routine.
furigana: のスキンケアをルーティンにする。

example.sentence: 早寝早起きの習慣をつけたい。
example.meaning: I want to form a habit of going to bed early and waking up early.
example.meaningKo: 일찍 자고 일찍 일어나는 습관을 들이고 싶다.

example.sentence: 勉強する時間を決めると、集中しやすい。
example.meaning: If you decide on a time to study, it is easier to concentrate.

example.sentence: 明確な目当てを持つと、やる気が出る。
example.meaning: Having a clear goal motivates you.

example.sentence: 毎日の体重を記録をつけている。
example.meaning: I keep a record of my weight every day.

example.sentence: 彼女の仕事ぶりはそつがない。
example.meaning: Her work is flawless.

example.sentence: 彼の準備には手抜かりがない。
example.meaning: His preparations have no oversights.

example.sentence: 抜かりなく準備を進めた。
example.meaning: I proceeded with preparations without any oversight.

example.sentence: 彼はその曲を完璧に演奏した。
example.meaning: He performed the piece perfectly.

example.sentence: 今回のプレゼンはほぼ完璧だった。
example.meaning: This presentation was almost perfect.

example.sentence: あと一歩で目標に届く。
example.meaning: I'm just one more step away from reaching my goal.
example.meaningKo: 한 발짝만 더 하면 목표에 닿는다.

119.meaning: a little more; just a bit more
119.subject: 표현

example.sentence: もう少し時間があれば完成できた。
example.meaning: If I had just a little more time, I could have finished it.
example.meaningKo: 조금만 더 시간이 있었다면 완성할 수 있었다.

119.meaning: just a tiny bit; only a little
119.subject: 표현

example.sentence: ほんの少し塩を加えるだけで味が変わる。
example.meaning: Adding just a tiny bit of salt changes the flavor.
example.meaningKo: 아주 조금 소금을 넣는 것만으로 맛이 달라진다.

example.sentence: 病気がずいぶんよくなった。
example.meaning: My illness has gotten much better.
furigana: がずいぶんよくなった。

119.meaning: so much that one can hardly recognize; remarkably
119.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 彼女は見違えるほど上手になった。
example.meaning: She has improved so much that you can hardly recognize her.
example.meaningKo: 그녀는 못 알아볼 정도로 실력이 늘었다.

example.sentence: 誕生日おめでとう！
example.meaning: Happy birthday!

example.sentence: 試験、がんばってください！
example.meaning: Please do your best on the exam!

example.sentence: 風邪ですか。おだいじに。
example.meaning: Do you have a cold? Please take care of yourself.

example.sentence: 道が濡れているから、きをつけて。
example.meaning: The road is wet, so please be careful.

example.sentence: じゃあ、またね！
example.meaning: Well then, see you later!

example.sentence: 田中さん、おひさしぶりです！お元気ですか。
example.meaning: Mr. Tanaka, it's been a long time! How are you?

example.sentence: ごめんください。山田さんはいらっしゃいますか。
example.meaning: Excuse me, is Mr. Yamada home?
furigana: さんはいらっしゃいますか。

120.meaning: Have a happy New Year! (year-end greeting)
120.subject: 인사 표현

example.sentence: 今年もお世話になりました。よいおとしを！
example.meaning: Thank you for everything this year. Have a happy New Year!
example.meaningKo: 올해도 신세 많이 졌습니다. 새해 복 많이 받으세요!

120.kanji: あけましておめでとう
120.subject: 인사 표현
furigana: あけましておめでとう

example.sentence: あけましておめでとう！今年もよろしく。
example.meaning: Happy New Year! I look forward to this year with you too.
example.meaningKo: 새해 복 많이 받아! 올해도 잘 부탁해.
furigana: あけましておめでとう

example.sentence: はじめまして。どうぞよろしく。
example.meaning: Nice to meet you. Please treat me well.

example.sentence: ものすごい速さで走った。
example.meaning: He ran at an incredible speed.

example.sentence: とてつもない量の宿題が出た。
example.meaning: An enormous amount of homework was assigned.

example.sentence: 彼女の歌がはんぱない。
example.meaning: Her singing is insanely good.

example.sentence: すさまじい嵐が町を襲った。
example.meaning: A tremendous storm struck the town.

example.sentence: おびただしい数の星が空に輝いていた。
example.meaning: An enormous number of stars were shining in the sky.
example.meaningKo: 수없이 많은 별이 하늘에 빛나고 있었다.

example.sentence: みごとな演技に観客は拍手を送った。
example.meaning: The audience applauded the splendid performance.

example.sentence: けなげな子供の姿に心を打たれた。
example.meaning: I was moved by the child's admirable figure.

example.sentence: いじらしい笑顔に思わず涙が出た。
example.meaning: I was moved to tears by that touchingly innocent smile.
example.meaningKo: 가엾도록 갸륵한 미소에 나도 모르게 눈물이 났다.

example.sentence: 友達が海外旅行に行くのがけなるい。
example.meaning: I'm envious that my friend is going on an overseas trip.

example.sentence: 子供たちの遊ぶ姿がほほえましい。
example.meaning: The sight of children playing is heartwarming.

example.sentence: 犯人について、こころあたりがある。
example.meaning: I have some idea about who the culprit is.
example.meaningKo: 범인에 대해 짐작 가는 데가 있다.

example.sentence: なぜ彼が怒っているのか、こころあたりがない。
example.meaning: I have no idea why he is angry.
example.meaningKo: 왜 그가 화가 났는지 짐작 가는 데가 없다.

example.sentence: 毎日の心がけが大切だ。
example.meaning: Daily mindset is important.

122.meaning: to keep in mind; to make an effort
122.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 健康に気をつけることを心がけている。
example.meaning: I keep in mind to take care of my health.
example.meaningKo: 건강에 신경 쓰는 것을 명심하고 있다.

example.sentence: 一人で旅に出るのは心細い。
example.meaning: Setting off on a trip alone is uneasy.

example.sentence: 頼りになる友達がいて心強い。
example.meaning: It is reassuring to have a reliable friend.
example.meaningKo: 믿을 수 있는 친구가 있어서 마음이 든든하다.

example.sentence: その町を離れるのは心残りだった。
example.meaning: Leaving that town left me with lingering regret.
example.meaningKo: 그 마을을 떠나는 것은 아쉬움이 남았다.

122.meaning: to move one's heart; to touch one's feelings
122.subject: 동사

example.sentence: その映画は多くの人の心を打った。
example.meaning: That movie moved the hearts of many people.
example.meaningKo: 그 영화는 많은 사람들의 마음을 감동시켰다.

122.meaning: to put one's heart into; to do something wholeheartedly
122.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 心を込めて手紙を書いた。
example.meaning: I wrote a letter wholeheartedly.

122.meaning: to open one's heart; to open up to someone
122.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 彼女はなかなか心を開かない。
example.meaning: She does not open up easily.

123.meaning: to require a lot of effort/care
123.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: この仕事は手がかかる。
example.meaning: This work requires a lot of effort.

example.sentence: 警察は事件の手がかりを見つけた。
example.meaning: The police found a clue to the case.

123.meaning: to be beyond control, impossible to deal with
123.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 彼の怒りは激しくて手がつけられない。
example.meaning: His anger is so intense that it is impossible to deal with.
example.meaningKo: 그의 분노가 너무 심해서 손을 댈 수 없다.

123.meaning: to be too busy to leave, unable to stop what one is doing
123.meaningKo: 손을 뗄 수 없다/바빠서 못 떠나다
123.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 今は手が離せないので、後で電話します。
example.meaning: I can't stop what I'm doing right now, so I'll call you later.
example.meaningKo: 지금은 손을 뗄 수 없어서 나중에 전화할게요.

123.meaning: to not have enough time or resources to handle everything
123.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 仕事が多くて細かいところまで手が回らない。
example.meaning: There is so much work that I can't attend to every detail.
example.meaningKo: 일이 많아서 세세한 곳까지 손이 미치지 못한다.

123.meaning: to be beyond one's ability to handle
123.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: この問題は私には手に余る。
example.meaning: This problem is beyond my ability to handle.
example.meaningKo: 이 문제는 나에게는 감당이 안 된다.

example.sentence: 彼はついにチケットを手に入れた。
example.meaning: He finally obtained the ticket.

123.meaning: to get hold of, to obtain
123.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 彼女は長年の夢を手にした。
example.meaning: She finally got hold of her long-cherished dream.

123.meaning: to pick up, to take in one's hand
123.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 彼は棚から本を手に取った。
example.meaning: He picked up a book from the shelf.

123.meaning: to become available, to be obtained
123.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: このブランドの商品はここでしか手に入らない。
example.meaning: This brand's products can only be obtained here.
example.meaningKo: 이 브랜드의 상품은 여기서만 구할 수 있다.

example.sentence: 彼はいつも上を目指している。
example.meaning: He is always aiming higher.
example.meaningKo: 그는 항상 위를 목표로 하고 있다.

example.sentence: 橋の上から下を見ると怖かった。
example.meaning: Looking down from the bridge was scary.

example.sentence: 困難があっても前に進むことが大切だ。
example.meaning: It is important to move forward even when facing difficulties.
example.meaningKo: 어려움이 있어도 앞으로 나아가는 것이 중요하다.

example.sentence: 時々後ろを振り返って、過去を見つめなおすことも必要だ。
example.meaning: It is also necessary to occasionally look back and reflect on the past.
example.meaningKo: 때때로 뒤를 돌아보고 과거를 다시 돌아보는 것도 필요하다.

example.sentence: 目標が決まったら、迷わずに進むべきだ。
example.meaning: Once your goal is set, you should move forward without hesitation.
example.meaningKo: 목표가 정해지면 망설이지 않고 나아가야 한다.

example.sentence: 彼女の努力が新しい道を開いた。
example.meaning: Her efforts opened up a new path.

example.sentence: 自分の道を選ぶのは自分自身だ。
example.meaning: It is you yourself who chooses your own path.
example.meaningKo: 자신의 길을 선택하는 것은 자기 자신이다.

example.sentence: 駅まで近道をすれば5分で着く。
example.meaning: If you take a shortcut to the station, you can get there in 5 minutes.

example.sentence: 工事中だったので、回り道をして帰った。
example.meaning: Since it was under construction, I took a detour to go home.
example.meaningKo: 공사 중이었기 때문에 돌아서 집에 갔다.

example.sentence: 夢を追うことは、いばらの道を歩くようなものだ。
example.meaning: Chasing a dream is like walking a thorny path.
example.meaningKo: 꿈을 좇는 것은 가시밭길을 걷는 것과 같다.

125.meaning: to be troubled / to rack one's brains
125.subject: 慣用句

example.sentence: 難しい問題に頭を抱える。
example.meaning: I rack my brains over a difficult problem.

125.meaning: to have a headache / to be worried
125.subject: 慣用句

example.sentence: 仕事のことを考えると頭が痛い。
example.meaning: Thinking about work gives me a headache.

125.meaning: to get angry / to lose one's temper
125.subject: 慣用句

example.sentence: 彼の態度に頭にくる。
example.meaning: His attitude makes me angry.

125.meaning: to use one's head / to think hard
125.subject: 慣用句

example.sentence: このゲームは頭を使う。
example.meaning: This game requires you to use your head.
